鞋.origin: 鞋 has leather 革 on the side; it means shoes.

鞘.origin: 鞘 has leather 革 on the side; it means the sheath or scabbard that holds a knife.

sentence.en: He puts the knife into its sheath.

鞠.origin: 鞠 has leather 革 on the side; it means to bow respectfully.

sentence.en: The student bows to the teacher.

鞣.origin: 鞣 joins leather 革 and soft 柔; it means to tan and soften hides into leather.

鞭.origin: 鞭 has leather 革 on the side; it means a whip.

韦.origin: 韦 once meant tanned leather; today it is mostly used as a family name.

韩.origin: 韩 is the short name for Korea, especially South Korea, and is also a family name.

sentence.en: He has been to South Korea.

韵.origin: 韵 joins sound 音 and even 匀; it means rhyme and a pleasing tone.

页.origin: 页 first drew a person's head and now means a page or sheet of paper.

sentence.en: Please turn to the next page.

顷.origin: 顷 means a brief moment; it is also a unit for measuring land.

sentence.en: In a moment the sky grew dark.

项.origin: 项 has head 页 on the side; it means the nape of the neck, and also an item.

顾.origin: 顾 has head 页 on the side; it means to turn the head and look back, or to care for.

sentence.en: The older sister takes care of her younger brother.

颇.origin: 颇 has head 页 on the side; it means rather or quite.

颈.origin: 颈 has head 页 on the side; it means the neck.

sentence.en: The giraffe's neck is very long.

颊.origin: 颊 puts head 页 with 夹; it means the cheeks on the sides of the face.

额.origin: 额 has head 页 on the side; it means the forehead, and also a fixed amount.

颠.origin: 颠 has head 页 on the side; it means the very top, or to bump and jolt.

sentence.en: He held the book upside down.

颤.origin: 颤 has head 页 on the side; it means to tremble and shiver.

飕.origin: 飕 has wind 风; it describes a chilly breeze and its whooshing sound.

sentence.en: The wind blows chilly and cool.

飞.origin: 飞 draws a bird flapping its wings; it means to fly.

食.origin: 食 shows a dish of food 良 with a lid 人 on top; it means food and to eat.

sentence.en: There is a lot of food on the table.

餐.origin: 餐 has food 食 at the bottom; it means a meal or to dine.

饥.origin: 饥 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means hungry.

饮.origin: 饮 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means to drink.

sentence.en: I want to drink a beverage.

饰.origin: 饰 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means to decorate or adorn.

饶.origin: 饶 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means plentiful, and also to forgive.

饷.origin: 饷 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means pay and rations for soldiers.

饼.origin: 饼 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means a flat cake or biscuit.

馅.origin: 馅 shows food 饣 stuffed into a hole 臽; it means the filling inside dumplings or buns.

sentence.en: This bun has a lot of filling.

馆.origin: 馆 joins food 饣 and official 官; it means a public building like a restaurant or library.

sentence.en: I go to the library to read.

馊.origin: 馊 joins food 饣 and old 叟; it means food that has gone spoiled and sour.

sentence.en: The rice has a spoiled smell.

馋.origin: 馋 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means greedy for tasty food.

sentence.en: Little brother is very fond of snacks.

馍.origin: 馍 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means steamed bread.

馏.origin: 馏 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it means to distill, or to resteam food.

馕.origin: 馕 has the food radical 饣 on the side; it is naan, a flat baked bread from Xinjiang.

sentence.en: Xinjiang's baked naan is very fragrant.

驮.origin: 驮 shows a big 大 load on a horse 马; it means to carry on the back.

word.en: to transport on an animal's back

sentence.en: The horse carries the goods on its back.

驱.origin: 驱 has horse 马 on the side; it means to drive forward or chase away.

驳.origin: 驳 has horse 马 on the side; it means mixed in color, and also to refute.

驴.origin: 驴 has horse 马 on the side; it means a donkey.

驶.origin: 驶 has horse 马 on the side; it means to drive or steer a vehicle.

驹.origin: 驹 has horse 马 on the side; it means a young colt.

驻.origin: 驻 has horse 马 on the side; it means to stop and stay stationed in a place.

sentence.en: The soldiers are stationed at the foot of the mountain.

骂.origin: 骂 shows two mouths 口口 above a horse 马; it means to scold or curse.

骚.origin: 骚 shows a flea 蚤 bothering a horse 马; it means to disturb or harass.

骟.origin: 骟 has horse 马 on the side; it means to geld or castrate an animal.

骡.origin: 骡 has horse 马 on the side; it means a mule, the child of a horse and a donkey.

骨.origin: 骨 shows bone 冎 with flesh ⺼; it means bone.

sentence.en: The puppy is gnawing a bone.

高.origin: 高 pictures a tall building; it means tall or high.

鬼.origin: 鬼 draws a figure with a strange head; it means a ghost.

sentence.en: There are ghosts and monsters in the story.

魂.origin: 魂 joins cloud 云 and ghost 鬼; it means the soul or spirit.
